Operation 'getPaymentMethodsForOrder' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context, missing_output_details. The original summary uses 'retrieve' and 'details' which are vague. The description lacks context about why payment methods are filtered (open payment amount consideration) and doesn't explain what is returned. Added clarity about the filtering logic, return value, and use case for AI agent decision-making.

data.current_summary: Retrieve payment instrument details for an order.
data.current_description: Gets the applicable payment methods for an existing order considering the open payment amount only.
data.summary_suggestion: Get applicable payment methods for an order
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the payment methods applicable to an existing order based on the remaining open payment amount. This operation filters available payment instruments by the outstanding balance that still needs to be paid. Returns a list of payment methods that can be used to complete payment for the order. Useful for determining which payment instruments are eligible for the remaining order balance.
data.reasoning: The original summary uses 'retrieve' and 'details' which are vague. The description lacks context about why payment methods are filtered (open payment amount consideration) and doesn't explain what is returned. Added clarity about the filtering logic, return value, and use case for AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'getTaxesFromOrder' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context, missing_output_details, not_actionable. Original summary is generic. Description is unclear about preconditions and return values, making it difficult for AI agents to determine when to use this operation. Added explicit precondition (external taxation must have been used), clarified what data is returned, and provided guidance on when the operation is applicable. Improved actionability by explaining the conditional nature of the operation.

data.current_summary: Retrieve external taxation data for an order.
data.current_description:
This method gives you the external taxation data of the order transferred from the basket during
order creation. This endpoint can be called only if external taxation was used. See POST /baskets
for more information.
data.summary_suggestion: Get external taxation data from order
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves external taxation data that was transferred to the order from the basket during order creation. This operation is only applicable when external taxation was configured and used during the order creation process. Returns the tax details including calculated tax amounts and applicable tax rates. If external taxation was not used, this endpoint will not return meaningful data. Refer to the basket creation documentation for details on configuring external taxation.
data.reasoning: Original summary is generic. Description is unclear about preconditions and return values, making it difficult for AI agents to determine when to use this operation. Added explicit precondition (external taxation must have been used), clarified what data is returned, and provided guidance on when the operation is applicable. Improved actionability by explaining the conditional nature of the operation.

message: Operation 'createOrder' has semantic quality issues: missing_context, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Description lacks clarity about what happens to the basket, what the operation returns, and the side effects of order creation. Added details about basket conversion, return value structure, and state changes to improve AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation.

data.current_summary: Submits an order for a basket.
data.current_description: Submits an order based on a prepared basket. The only considered value from the request body is basketId.
data.description_suggestion: Creates an order from a prepared basket. The operation accepts a basket ID and converts it into an order, processing all basket contents, pricing, and adjustments. Only the basketId field from the request body is used; other fields are ignored. Returns the created Order object with order number, items, pricing, and payment status. This operation transitions the basket state and generates an order number for tracking and fulfillment.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity about what happens to the basket, what the operation returns, and the side effects of order creation. Added details about basket conversion, return value structure, and state changes to improve AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation.

message: Operation 'getOrder' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context. Original summary and description are too generic and lack specificity. Enhanced summary to include 'by order number' for clarity. Expanded description to specify what order details are returned, authentication requirements, and that it's a read-only operation to help AI agents understand the operation's scope and when to use it.

data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve order details by order number.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves complete order information including order status, items, pricing, shipments, payment instruments, and addresses for a specified order number. Requires authentication with shopper access token. Returns the full Order object with all order details. This operation is read-only and does not modify order state.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are too generic and lack specificity. Enhanced summary to include 'by order number' for clarity. Expanded description to specify what order details are returned, authentication requirements, and that it's a read-only operation to help AI agents understand the operation's scope and when to use it.

message: Operation 'failOrder' has semantic quality issues: missing_context, missing_output_details, missing_input_details. Original description mentions key details but lacks clarity about what inputs are required, what the operation returns, and the business context. Enhanced summary to include basket reopening. Expanded description to clarify reasonCode requirement, return value, typical use cases, and the purpose of basket reopening for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description:
Fails an unplaced order and optionally reopens the basket when indicated.
Creates a HistoryEntry in the failed Order with provided reasonCode.
data.summary_suggestion: Fail an unplaced order and optionally reopen basket.
data.description_suggestion: Fails an unplaced order and optionally reopens the associated basket for reuse. Requires a reasonCode to be provided in the request, which is recorded as a HistoryEntry in the order for audit purposes. Returns the updated Order object with failed status and history entry. This operation is typically used to cancel orders that have not yet been placed or processed. The basket reopening allows customers to modify and resubmit their order.
data.reasoning: Original description mentions key details but lacks clarity about what inputs are required, what the operation returns, and the business context. Enhanced summary to include basket reopening. Expanded description to clarify reasonCode requirement, return value, typical use cases, and the purpose of basket reopening for better AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'createPaymentInstrumentForOrder' has semantic quality issues: missing_context, missing_output_details. Description lacks clarity about what is returned and important context about masked card storage. Added return value details, clarification about masked card storage (important security context from API overview), and note about multiple instruments to improve AI agent understanding of operation capabilities and constraints.

data.current_summary: Add a payment instrument to an order.
data.current_description:
Adds a payment instrument to an order.

Details:

The payment instrument is added with the provided details. The payment method must be applicable for the order see GET
/baskets/{basketId}/payment-methods, if the payment method is 'CREDIT_CARD' a paymentCard must be specified in the request.
data.description_suggestion: Adds a payment instrument to an order with the provided payment details. The payment method must be applicable for the order (validate using GET /baskets/{basketId}/payment-methods). For CREDIT_CARD payment methods, a paymentCard object must be included in the request. The API stores only masked credit card numbers, not full card data. Returns the created PaymentInstrument object with payment method, amount, and masked card details if applicable. Multiple payment instruments can be added to a single order.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity about what is returned and important context about masked card storage. Added return value details, clarification about masked card storage (important security context from API overview), and note about multiple instruments to improve AI agent understanding of operation capabilities and constraints.

message: Operation 'removePaymentInstrumentFromOrder' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description is too brief and lacks important context. Enhanced description to clarify what is removed, that it's irreversible, what the response indicates, side effects when removing the only payment instrument, and typical use cases to help AI agents understand when and why to use this operation.

data.current_summary: Remove a payment instrument from an order.
data.current_description: Removes a payment instrument of an order.
data.description_suggestion: Removes a specified payment instrument from an order by payment instrument ID. This operation deletes the payment instrument association with the order and cannot be undone. Returns a 200 status on successful removal. If the removed instrument was the only payment method on the order, the order will have no payment instruments until a new one is added. Use this operation to correct payment information or remove invalid payment methods before order processing.
data.reasoning: Original description is too brief and lacks important context. Enhanced description to clarify what is removed, that it's irreversible, what the response indicates, side effects when removing the only payment instrument, and typical use cases to help AI agents understand when and why to use this operation.

message: Operation 'updatePaymentInstrumentForOrder' has semantic quality issues: missing_context, missing_output_details. Description lacks clarity about what is returned, whether updates are partial or full, and typical use cases. Added return value details, clarification about partial update support, masked card storage context, and use case guidance to improve AI agent understanding of operation behavior and when to apply it.

data.current_summary: Update payment instrument details for an order.
data.current_description:
Updates a payment instrument of an order.

Details:

The payment instrument is updated with the provided details. The payment method must be applicable for the
order see GET /baskets/{basketId}/payment-methods, if the payment method is 'CREDIT_CARD' a
paymentCard must be specified in the request.
data.description_suggestion: Updates an existing payment instrument on an order with new payment details. The payment method must be applicable for the order (validate using GET /baskets/{basketId}/payment-methods). For CREDIT_CARD payment methods, a paymentCard object must be included in the request. The API stores only masked credit card numbers. Supports partial updates - only provided fields are modified. Returns the updated PaymentInstrument object with new payment method, amount, and masked card details if applicable. Use this operation to correct payment information or change payment methods before order processing.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity about what is returned, whether updates are partial or full, and typical use cases. Added return value details, clarification about partial update support, masked card storage context, and use case guidance to improve AI agent understanding of operation behavior and when to apply it.
